Conditional Formatting

Complete documentation for conditional formatting in Aspose.Cells FOSS for .NET

← Back to Index

Conditional Formatting Overview

Conditional formatting allows you to automatically format cells based on their values or formulas. This powerful feature helps highlight important data, identify trends, and make your spreadsheets more visually informative and easier to understand.

Key Features:
  • Highlight cells based on value comparisons (greater than, less than, between, etc.)
  • Apply data bars, color scales, and icon sets for visual data analysis
  • Use formulas for complex conditional logic
  • Apply different styles based on multiple conditions
  • Create top/bottom rules (top 10, bottom 10, above average, etc.)
  • Duplicate value highlighting
  • Text contains specific strings

Condition Types

Cell Value Conditions
// Create condition based on cell value
var condition = new FormatCondition();
condition.Type = ConditionType.CellValue;
condition.Operator = OperatorType.GreaterThan;
condition.Formula1 = "100";
condition.Style.ForegroundColor = System.Drawing.Color.Green;
condition.Style.Pattern = BackgroundType.Solid;
Expression Conditions
// Create condition based on formula/expression
var condition = new FormatCondition();
condition.Type = ConditionType.Expression;
condition.Formula1 = "=MOD(A1, 2) = 0";
condition.Style.ForegroundColor = System.Drawing.Color.LightBlue;
condition.Style.Pattern = BackgroundType.Solid;
Comparison Operators
// Available operators for cell value conditions:
// - Equal
// - NotEqual
// - GreaterThan
// - LessThan
// - GreaterThanOrEqual
// - LessThanOrEqual
// - Between
// - NotBetween

condition.Operator = OperatorType.GreaterThan;
condition.Operator = OperatorType.Between;
condition.Operator = OperatorType.Equal;
// ... and more

Basic Conditional Formatting

Highlight Values Greater Than
// Highlight values greater than 100
var condition = new FormatCondition();
condition.Type = ConditionType.CellValue;
condition.Operator = OperatorType.GreaterThan;
condition.Formula1 = "100";
condition.Style.ForegroundColor = System.Drawing.Color.LightGreen;
condition.Style.Pattern = BackgroundType.Solid;

worksheet.ConditionalFormattings.Add(cells.CreateRange(0, 0, 10, 1), condition);
Highlight Values Between Range
// Highlight values between 50 and 100
var condition = new FormatCondition();
condition.Type = ConditionType.CellValue;
condition.Operator = OperatorType.Between;
condition.Formula1 = "50";
condition.Formula2 = "100";
condition.Style.ForegroundColor = System.Drawing.Color.Yellow;
condition.Style.Pattern = BackgroundType.Solid;

worksheet.ConditionalFormattings.Add(cells.CreateRange(0, 1, 10, 1), condition);
Highlight Equal Values
// Highlight cells equal to a specific value
var condition = new FormatCondition();
condition.Type = ConditionType.CellValue;
condition.Operator = OperatorType.Equal;
condition.Formula1 = "\"Complete\"";
condition.Style.ForegroundColor = System.Drawing.Color.Green;
condition.Style.Font.Color = System.Drawing.Color.White;
condition.Style.Font.Bold = true;
condition.Style.Pattern = BackgroundType.Solid;

worksheet.ConditionalFormattings.Add(cells.CreateRange(0, 2, 10, 1), condition);
Highlight Top Values
// Highlight top 10 values
var condition = new FormatCondition();
condition.Type = ConditionType.Top10;
condition.Operator = OperatorType.Top10;
condition.Style.ForegroundColor = System.Drawing.Color.Blue;
condition.Style.Font.Color = System.Drawing.Color.White;
condition.Style.Font.Bold = true;
condition.Style.Pattern = BackgroundType.Solid;

worksheet.ConditionalFormattings.Add(cells.CreateRange(0, 0, 20, 1), condition);
Highlight Above Average
// Highlight values above average
var condition = new FormatCondition();
condition.Type = ConditionType.AboveAverage;
condition.Operator = OperatorType.AboveAverage;
condition.Style.ForegroundColor = System.Drawing.Color.LightBlue;
condition.Style.Pattern = BackgroundType.Solid;

worksheet.ConditionalFormattings.Add(cells.CreateRange(0, 1, 20, 1), condition);

Formula-Based Formatting

Even/Odd Numbers
// Highlight even numbers
var evenCondition = new FormatCondition();
evenCondition.Type = ConditionType.Expression;
evenCondition.Formula1 = "=MOD(A1, 2) = 0";
evenCondition.Style.ForegroundColor = System.Drawing.Color.LightBlue;
evenCondition.Style.Pattern = BackgroundType.Solid;

// Highlight odd numbers
var oddCondition = new FormatCondition();
oddCondition.Type = ConditionType.Expression;
oddCondition.Formula1 = "=MOD(A1, 2) <> 0";
oddCondition.Style.ForegroundColor = System.Drawing.Color.LightGreen;
oddCondition.Style.Pattern = BackgroundType.Solid;

worksheet.ConditionalFormattings.Add(cells.CreateRange(0, 0, 10, 1), evenCondition);
worksheet.ConditionalFormattings.Add(cells.CreateRange(0, 0, 10, 1), oddCondition);
Duplicate Values
// Highlight duplicate values
var condition = new FormatCondition();
condition.Type = ConditionType.Expression;
condition.Formula1 = "=COUNTIF($A$1:$A$10, A1) > 1";
condition.Style.ForegroundColor = System.Drawing.Color.Red;
condition.Style.Font.Color = System.Drawing.Color.White;
condition.Style.Font.Bold = true;
condition.Style.Pattern = BackgroundType.Solid;

worksheet.ConditionalFormattings.Add(cells.CreateRange(0, 0, 10, 1), condition);
Unique Values
// Highlight unique values
var condition = new FormatCondition();
condition.Type = ConditionType.Expression;
condition.Formula1 = "=COUNTIF($A$1:$A$10, A1) = 1";
condition.Style.ForegroundColor = System.Drawing.Color.LightYellow;
condition.Style.Pattern = BackgroundType.Solid;

worksheet.ConditionalFormattings.Add(cells.CreateRange(0, 0, 10, 1), condition);
Text Contains
// Highlight cells containing specific text
var condition = new FormatCondition();
condition.Type = ConditionType.Expression;
condition.Formula1 = "=ISNUMBER(FIND(\"urgent\", A1))";
condition.Style.ForegroundColor = System.Drawing.Color.Red;
condition.Style.Font.Color = System.Drawing.Color.White;
condition.Style.Font.Bold = true;
condition.Style.Pattern = BackgroundType.Solid;

worksheet.ConditionalFormattings.Add(cells.CreateRange(0, 0, 20, 1), condition);
Date-Based Conditions
// Highlight dates within last 7 days
var recentCondition = new FormatCondition();
recentCondition.Type = ConditionType.Expression;
recentCondition.Formula1 = "=AND(A1 > TODAY() - 7, A1 <= TODAY())";
recentCondition.Style.ForegroundColor = System.Drawing.Color.LightGreen;
recentCondition.Style.Pattern = BackgroundType.Solid;

// Highlight dates older than 30 days
var oldCondition = new FormatCondition();
oldCondition.Type = ConditionType.Expression;
oldCondition.Formula1 = "=A1 < TODAY() - 30";
oldCondition.Style.ForegroundColor = System.Drawing.Color.Gray;
oldCondition.Style.Font.Color = System.Drawing.Color.White;
oldCondition.Style.Pattern = BackgroundType.Solid;

worksheet.ConditionalFormattings.Add(cells.CreateRange(0, 2, 20, 1), recentCondition);
worksheet.ConditionalFormattings.Add(cells.CreateRange(0, 2, 20, 1), oldCondition);

Advanced Conditional Formatting

Multiple Conditions
// Apply multiple conditions to the same range
var highCondition = new FormatCondition();
highCondition.Type = ConditionType.CellValue;
highCondition.Operator = OperatorType.GreaterThan;
highCondition.Formula1 = "1000";
highCondition.Style.ForegroundColor = System.Drawing.Color.Red;
highCondition.Style.Font.Color = System.Drawing.Color.White;
highCondition.Style.Font.Bold = true;
highCondition.Style.Pattern = BackgroundType.Solid;

var mediumCondition = new FormatCondition();
mediumCondition.Type = ConditionType.CellValue;
mediumCondition.Operator = OperatorType.Between;
mediumCondition.Formula1 = "500";
mediumCondition.Formula2 = "1000";
mediumCondition.Style.ForegroundColor = System.Drawing.Color.Yellow;
mediumCondition.Style.Pattern = BackgroundType.Solid;

var lowCondition = new FormatCondition();
lowCondition.Type = ConditionType.CellValue;
lowCondition.Operator = OperatorType.LessThan;
lowCondition.Formula1 = "500";
lowCondition.Style.ForegroundColor = System.Drawing.Color.Green;
lowCondition.Style.Font.Color = System.Drawing.Color.White;
lowCondition.Style.Pattern = BackgroundType.Solid;

// Apply all conditions to the same range
var range = cells.CreateRange(0, 0, 10, 1);
worksheet.ConditionalFormattings.Add(range, highCondition);
worksheet.ConditionalFormattings.Add(range, mediumCondition);
worksheet.ConditionalFormattings.Add(range, lowCondition);
Row-Based Formatting
// Highlight entire row based on cell value
var condition = new FormatCondition();
condition.Type = ConditionType.Expression;
condition.Formula1 = "=$C1 > 1000";  // Column C determines highlighting
condition.Style.ForegroundColor = System.Drawing.Color.LightBlue;
condition.Style.Pattern = BackgroundType.Solid;

// Apply to entire row range
worksheet.ConditionalFormattings.Add(cells.CreateRange(0, 0, 10, 5), condition);
Data Validation Status
// Highlight cells that fail validation (simple example)
var condition = new FormatCondition();
condition.Type = ConditionType.Expression;
condition.Formula1 = "=OR(A1 < 0, A1 > 100)";
condition.Style.ForegroundColor = System.Drawing.Color.Red;
condition.Style.Font.Color = System.Drawing.Color.White;
condition.Style.Pattern = BackgroundType.Solid;

worksheet.ConditionalFormattings.Add(cells.CreateRange(0, 0, 10, 1), condition);

Managing Conditional Formatting

Accessing and Modifying
// Access conditional formatting collection
var formatting = worksheet.ConditionalFormattings;

// Get count
int count = formatting.Count;

// Access specific formatting by index
var firstFormat = formatting[0];

// Remove formatting
formatting.RemoveAt(0);

// Clear all formatting
formatting.Clear();
Condition Priority
// Conditions are evaluated in the order they are added
// First matching condition is applied

// Add higher priority conditions first
var urgentCondition = new FormatCondition();
urgentCondition.Type = ConditionType.Expression;
urgentCondition.Formula1 = "=$B1 = \"Urgent\"";
urgentCondition.Style.ForegroundColor = System.Drawing.Color.Red;
urgentCondition.Style.Pattern = BackgroundType.Solid;

var normalCondition = new FormatCondition();
normalCondition.Type = ConditionType.Expression;
normalCondition.Formula1 = "=$B1 = \"Normal\"";
normalCondition.Style.ForegroundColor = System.Drawing.Color.Green;
normalCondition.Style.Pattern = BackgroundType.Solid;

// Add in order of priority
worksheet.ConditionalFormattings.Add(range, urgentCondition);   // Higher priority
worksheet.ConditionalFormattings.Add(range, normalCondition);   // Lower priority

Complete Example

Sales Performance Dashboard
using Aspose.Cells_FOSS;
using System;

class Program
{
    static void Main()
    {
        var workbook = new Workbook();
        var worksheet = workbook.Worksheets[0];
        var cells = worksheet.Cells;

        // Add header
        cells["A1"].PutValue("Sales Person");
        cells["B1"].PutValue("Region");
        cells["C1"].PutValue("Sales");
        cells["D1"].PutValue("Target");
        cells["E1"].PutValue("Achievement %");

        // Add sample data
        string[] salespeople = { "John", "Jane", "Bob", "Alice", "Charlie", "Diana" };
        string[] regions = { "East", "West", "North", "South", "East", "West" };
        int[] sales = { 12000, 8500, 15000, 9200, 11000, 7500 };
        int[] targets = { 10000, 10000, 10000, 10000, 10000, 10000 };

        for (int i = 0; i < salespeople.Length; i++)
        {
            cells[i + 2, 0].PutValue(salespeople[i]);
            cells[i + 2, 1].PutValue(regions[i]);
            cells[i + 2, 2].PutValue(sales[i]);
            cells[i + 2, 3].PutValue(targets[i]);
            cells[i + 2, 4].Formula = $"=C{i + 2}/D{i + 2}*100";
        }

        // Apply header styling
        var headerStyle = workbook.CreateStyle();
        headerStyle.Font.Bold = true;
        headerStyle.Font.Color = System.Drawing.Color.White;
        headerStyle.BackgroundColor = System.Drawing.Color.FromArgb(0, 51, 153);
        for (int col = 0; col <= 4; col++)
        {
            cells[0, col].SetStyle(headerStyle);
        }

        // Conditional formatting for achievement > 100% (Green)
        var aboveTargetCondition = new FormatCondition();
        aboveTargetCondition.Type = ConditionType.CellValue;
        aboveTargetCondition.Operator = OperatorType.GreaterThan;
        aboveTargetCondition.Formula1 = "100";
        aboveTargetCondition.Style.ForegroundColor = System.Drawing.Color.LightGreen;
        aboveTargetCondition.Style.Pattern = BackgroundType.Solid;

        worksheet.ConditionalFormattings.Add(cells.CreateRange(1, 4, 6, 1), aboveTargetCondition);

        // Conditional formatting for achievement 90-100% (Yellow)
        var nearTargetCondition = new FormatCondition();
        nearTargetCondition.Type = ConditionType.CellValue;
        nearTargetCondition.Operator = OperatorType.Between;
        nearTargetCondition.Formula1 = "90";
        nearTargetCondition.Formula2 = "100";
        nearTargetCondition.Style.ForegroundColor = System.Drawing.Color.Yellow;
        nearTargetCondition.Style.Pattern = BackgroundType.Solid;

        worksheet.ConditionalFormattings.Add(cells.CreateRange(1, 4, 6, 1), nearTargetCondition);

        // Conditional formatting for achievement < 90% (Red)
        var belowTargetCondition = new FormatCondition();
        belowTargetCondition.Type = ConditionType.CellValue;
        belowTargetCondition.Operator = OperatorType.LessThan;
        belowTargetCondition.Formula1 = "90";
        belowTargetCondition.Style.ForegroundColor = System.Drawing.Color.Red;
        belowTargetCondition.Style.Font.Color = System.Drawing.Color.White;
        belowTargetCondition.Style.Pattern = BackgroundType.Solid;

        worksheet.ConditionalFormattings.Add(cells.CreateRange(1, 4, 6, 1), belowTargetCondition);

        // Highlight top performer
        var topCondition = new FormatCondition();
        topCondition.Type = ConditionType.Expression;
        topCondition.Formula1 = "=$C2 = MAX($C$2:$C$7)";
        topCondition.Style.ForegroundColor = System.Drawing.Color.Blue;
        topCondition.Style.Font.Color = System.Drawing.Color.White;
        topCondition.Style.Font.Bold = true;
        topCondition.Style.Pattern = BackgroundType.Solid;

        worksheet.ConditionalFormattings.Add(cells.CreateRange(1, 0, 6, 5), topCondition);

        // Apply currency formatting
        var currencyStyle = workbook.CreateStyle();
        currencyStyle.NumberFormat = "$#,##0";
        for (int row = 2; row <= 7; row++)
        {
            cells[row, 2].SetStyle(currencyStyle);
            cells[row, 3].SetStyle(currencyStyle);
        }

        // Apply percentage formatting
        var percentStyle = workbook.CreateStyle();
        percentStyle.NumberFormat = "0.0%";
        for (int row = 2; row <= 7; row++)
        {
            cells[row, 4].SetStyle(percentStyle);
        }

        // Auto-fit columns
        worksheet.AutoFitColumns();

        workbook.Save("sales-dashboard.xlsx");
        Console.WriteLine("Sales performance dashboard with conditional formatting created!");
    }
}
Project Status Tracker
using Aspose.Cells_FOSS;
using System;

class Program
{
    static void Main()
    {
        var workbook = new Workbook();
        var worksheet = workbook.Worksheets[0];
        var cells = worksheet.Cells;

        // Create header
        cells["A1"].PutValue("Project");
        cells["B1"].PutValue("Status");
        cells["C1"].PutValue("Due Date");
        cells["D1"].PutValue("Priority");

        // Add sample projects
        string[] projects = { "Website Redesign", "Mobile App", "Database Migration", "API Integration", "Testing" };
        string[] statuses = { "Complete", "In Progress", "Pending", "In Progress", "Complete" };
        DateTime[] dueDates = {
            DateTime.Now.AddDays(-10),
            DateTime.Now.AddDays(5),
            DateTime.Now.AddDays(20),
            DateTime.Now.AddDays(-5),
            DateTime.Now.AddDays(-15)
        };
        string[] priorities = { "High", "Medium", "Low", "High", "Medium" };

        for (int i = 0; i < projects.Length; i++)
        {
            cells[i + 2, 0].PutValue(projects[i]);
            cells[i + 2, 1].PutValue(statuses[i]);
            cells[i + 2, 2].PutValue(dueDates[i]);
            cells[i + 2, 3].PutValue(priorities[i]);
        }

        // Status-based conditional formatting
        var completeCondition = new FormatCondition();
        completeCondition.Type = ConditionType.CellValue;
        completeCondition.Operator = OperatorType.Equal;
        completeCondition.Formula1 = "\"Complete\"";
        completeCondition.Style.ForegroundColor = System.Drawing.Color.LightGreen;
        completeCondition.Style.Pattern = BackgroundType.Solid;

        worksheet.ConditionalFormattings.Add(cells.CreateRange(1, 1, 5, 1), completeCondition);

        var progressCondition = new FormatCondition();
        progressCondition.Type = ConditionType.CellValue;
        progressCondition.Operator = OperatorType.Equal;
        progressCondition.Formula1 = "\"In Progress\"";
        progressCondition.Style.ForegroundColor = System.Drawing.Color.Yellow;
        progressCondition.Style.Pattern = BackgroundType.Solid;

        worksheet.ConditionalFormattings.Add(cells.CreateRange(1, 1, 5, 1), progressCondition);

        var pendingCondition = new FormatCondition();
        pendingCondition.Type = ConditionType.CellValue;
        pendingCondition.Operator = OperatorType.Equal;
        pendingCondition.Formula1 = "\"Pending\"";
        pendingCondition.Style.ForegroundColor = System.Drawing.Color.Red;
        pendingCondition.Style.Font.Color = System.Drawing.Color.White;
        pendingCondition.Style.Pattern = BackgroundType.Solid;

        worksheet.ConditionalFormattings.Add(cells.CreateRange(1, 1, 5, 1), pendingCondition);

        // Date-based conditional formatting
        var overdueCondition = new FormatCondition();
        overdueCondition.Type = ConditionType.Expression;
        overdueCondition.Formula1 = "=AND(C2 < TODAY(), B2 <> \"Complete\")";
        overdueCondition.Style.ForegroundColor = System.Drawing.Color.Red;
        overdueCondition.Style.Font.Color = System.Drawing.Color.White;
        overdueCondition.Style.Font.Bold = true;
        overdueCondition.Style.Pattern = BackgroundType.Solid;

        worksheet.ConditionalFormattings.Add(cells.CreateRange(1, 0, 5, 4), overdueCondition);

        // Priority-based conditional formatting
        var highPriorityCondition = new FormatCondition();
        highPriorityCondition.Type = ConditionType.CellValue;
        highPriorityCondition.Operator = OperatorType.Equal;
        highPriorityCondition.Formula1 = "\"High\"";
        highPriorityCondition.Style.ForegroundColor = System.Drawing.Color.Red;
        highPriorityCondition.Style.Font.Color = System.Drawing.Color.White;
        highPriorityCondition.Style.Font.Bold = true;
        highPriorityCondition.Style.Pattern = BackgroundType.Solid;

        worksheet.ConditionalFormattings.Add(cells.CreateRange(1, 3, 5, 1), highPriorityCondition);

        var mediumPriorityCondition = new FormatCondition();
        mediumPriorityCondition.Type = ConditionType.CellValue;
        mediumPriorityCondition.Operator = OperatorType.Equal;
        mediumPriorityCondition.Formula1 = "\"Medium\"";
        mediumPriorityCondition.Style.ForegroundColor = System.Drawing.Color.Yellow;
        mediumPriorityCondition.Style.Pattern = BackgroundType.Solid;

        worksheet.ConditionalFormattings.Add(cells.CreateRange(1, 3, 5, 1), mediumPriorityCondition);

        var lowPriorityCondition = new FormatCondition();
        lowPriorityCondition.Type = ConditionType.CellValue;
        lowPriorityCondition.Operator = OperatorType.Equal;
        lowPriorityCondition.Formula1 = "\"Low\"";
        lowPriorityCondition.Style.ForegroundColor = System.Drawing.Color.LightGreen;
        lowPriorityCondition.Style.Pattern = BackgroundType.Solid;

        worksheet.ConditionalFormattings.Add(cells.CreateRange(1, 3, 5, 1), lowPriorityCondition);

        // Apply header styling
        var headerStyle = workbook.CreateStyle();
        headerStyle.Font.Bold = true;
        headerStyle.Font.Color = System.Drawing.Color.White;
        headerStyle.BackgroundColor = System.Drawing.Color.FromArgb(0, 51, 153);
        for (int col = 0; col <= 3; col++)
        {
            cells[0, col].SetStyle(headerStyle);
        }

        // Auto-fit columns
        worksheet.AutoFitColumns();

        workbook.Save("project-tracker.xlsx");
        Console.WriteLine("Project status tracker with conditional formatting created!");
    }
}

Important Notes

  • Priority Order: Conditions are evaluated in the order they are added. First matching condition is applied.
  • Formula References: In formulas, use relative references (A1) not absolute references ($A$1) for cell-specific conditions.
  • Range Coverage: Conditional formatting applies to the entire specified range, even if not all cells meet the condition.
  • Performance: Too many conditional formatting rules can impact workbook performance.
  • Style Conflicts: When multiple conditions match, only the first matching condition's style is applied.
  • Excel Compatibility: All conditional formatting rules are fully compatible with Microsoft Excel.
  • Complex Formulas: Keep formulas simple for better performance and readability.
